explanation: An aurora dances above the cold waters of Lake Superior during a quiet night in late winter. The green and crimson curtains were triggered by a coronal mass ejection that swept past Earth, energizing oxygen and nitrogen high in the atmosphere. This short video compresses nearly three hours of shimmering activity into a few seconds.
title: Aurora over Lake Superior

explanation: At only 2.5 million light-years distant, the Andromeda Galaxy (M31) is our nearest large galactic neighbor and the most distant object easily visible to the unaided eye. This deep mosaic reveals dust lanes, blue star-forming regions, and the galaxy's two bright satellite galaxies, M32 and M110.
title: M31: The Andromeda Galaxy

explanation: Comet C/2023 A3 (Tsuchinshan-ATLAS) trails a long ion tail across the southern sky in this long-exposure capture made from a remote ranch in Patagonia. The comet, discovered independently by observatories in China and South Africa, made its closest approach to the Sun before swinging back toward the outer solar system.
title: A Comet over Patagonia

explanation: Sunspot group AR3614 sprawled across the solar disk during a peak in the Sun's 11-year activity cycle. Captured in extreme ultraviolet light, the active region shows tangled magnetic loops where charged plasma is funneled along field lines, occasionally erupting as solar flares and coronal mass ejections.
title: Sunspot Group AR3614

explanation: The James Webb Space Telescope returns a layered, near-infrared view of the Pillars of Creation in the Eagle Nebula (M16). Webb's instruments pierce much of the obscuring dust, revealing newborn stars embedded in the dense columns of cold gas some 6,500 light-years away.
title: The Pillars of Creation from Webb

explanation: A panorama assembled from Curiosity Mastcam frames looks back across the foothills of Mount Sharp inside Gale crater. Layered sedimentary rock records billions of years of Martian history, including an ancient era when liquid water may have pooled in the crater floor.
title: Gale Crater Foothills from Curiosity

explanation: An annular solar eclipse paints a ring of fire above the Colorado Rockies as the Moon, near apogee, passes directly in front of the Sun without fully covering it. A careful sequence of filtered exposures captures the progression from first contact through the brilliant ring and back again.
title: Annular Eclipse over the Rockies

explanation: The central band of the Milky Way arcs over the high desert telescopes of Chile's Atacama region. At this altitude the air is thin and exceptionally dry, making it one of the darkest and clearest skies on Earth for ground-based astronomy.
title: The Milky Way over the Atacama

explanation: Jupiter and its four large Galilean moons gleam in this high-resolution amateur image taken during a night of steady seeing. The Great Red Spot, a storm larger than Earth, is rotating into view along with intricate cloud belts churned by the giant planet's powerful winds.
title: Jupiter and the Galilean Moons

explanation: A total lunar eclipse turns the Moon a deep coppery red as it passes fully through Earth's shadow. The color comes from sunlight bent and filtered through our planet's atmosphere, the same effect that reddens sunrises and sunsets, projected onto the lunar surface.
title: A Total Lunar Eclipse

explanation: The Orion Nebula (M42) is a vast stellar nursery just 1,350 light-years away, visible to the naked eye as the fuzzy middle star in Orion's sword. This deep exposure captures glowing hydrogen, intricate dust, and the four hot Trapezium stars whose radiation lights the entire cloud.
title: The Great Nebula in Orion

explanation: The Carina Nebula stretches across this wide-field view from the European Southern Observatory in Chile. Within its glowing clouds lies the unstable, luminous star Eta Carinae, which may end its life in a supernova explosion visible even in daylight.
title: The Carina Nebula in Detail

explanation: The Whirlpool Galaxy (M51) and its smaller companion NGC 5195 are locked in a gravitational dance 31 million light-years away. The interaction has compressed gas in the larger spiral, triggering waves of star formation that trace its graceful, well-defined arms.
title: M51: The Whirlpool Galaxy

explanation: A brilliant Geminid meteor streaks above a lone observer on a clear December night. The annual Geminid shower, produced by debris from the rock comet 3200 Phaethon, is among the most reliable of the year, often delivering more than one bright meteor per minute at its peak.
title: Geminid Meteors over the Desert

explanation: Saturn's rings cast crisp shadows across the planet's cloud tops in this Cassini portrait. The countless icy particles that make up the rings range from dust grains to house-sized chunks, all orbiting in a band thousands of kilometers wide but in places only tens of meters thick.
title: Saturn and Its Rings from Cassini

explanation: The faint band of the zodiacal light rises like a pyramid from the horizon after dusk, formed by sunlight scattering off dust grains spread throughout the inner solar system. This subtle glow is best seen from dark sites far from city lights in the weeks around the equinox.
title: Zodiacal Light over the Mountains

explanation: Webb's mid-infrared instrument reveals the dusty cocoon surrounding the supernova remnant Cassiopeia A. The expanding shell of debris, all that remains of a star that exploded about 340 years ago as seen from Earth, glows with newly forged heavy elements seeding the next generation of stars.
title: Cassiopeia A from Webb

explanation: A complete circular rainbow seen from a high vantage point arcs over an island in the Indian Ocean. Although rainbows always form full circles, the lower half is usually hidden by the ground; from a mountaintop or aircraft the entire ring can sometimes be glimpsed.
title: A Full Circle Rainbow

explanation: Juno's JunoCam captured this swirling view of Jupiter's turbulent southern hemisphere during a close flyby. Cyclonic storms, white oval anticyclones, and looping bands of ammonia clouds reveal the restless dynamics of the largest planet's deep atmosphere.
title: Jupiter's Clouds from Juno

explanation: This composite analemma traces the Sun's figure-eight path across the sky over the course of a year, photographed at the same time each clear day from a single location in Turkey. The shape arises from Earth's axial tilt and the varying speed of its elliptical orbit.
title: A Solar Analemma over a Year

explanation: The Large Magellanic Cloud, a satellite galaxy of our own Milky Way, hangs in the southern sky some 160,000 light-years away. This deep image highlights the Tarantula Nebula, the most active star-forming region known in the Local Group of galaxies.
title: The Large Magellanic Cloud

explanation: Venus blazes as the brilliant evening star above a coastal lighthouse in Portugal, paired with a slender crescent Moon. As the second planet from the Sun, Venus is perpetually shrouded in reflective clouds of sulfuric acid, making it the brightest natural object in the night sky after the Moon.
title: Venus and the Crescent Moon
